title: URIs should not be hardcoded
description: Hard-coding a URI makes it difficult to test a program for a variety of reasons: path literals are not always portable across operating systems a given absolute path may not exist in a specific test environment a specified Internet URL may not be available when executing the tests production environment filesystems usually differ from the development environment In addition, hard-coded URIs can contain sensitive information, like IP addresses, and they should not be stored in the code. For all those reasons, a URI should never be hard coded. Instead, it should be replaced by a customizable parameter.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: URIs should not be hardcoded - use configuration instead

logic.reasoning: Hardcoded URIs create problems for portability, testing, and security. Absolute paths may not exist in test environments, URLs may not be available during tests, and sensitive information like IP addresses should not be in code. Use configuration instead.
